differential_diagnoses: Nephrotic syndrome secondary to IgA-dominant nephropathy or membranous nephropathy
Decompensated heart failure with preserved ejection fraction (HFpEF)
Infectious etiology (e.g., chronic parasitic infection or subacute bacterial endocarditis)
next_steps: Perform 24-hour urine collection for total protein and creatinine clearance
Perform ultrasound-guided renal biopsy with immunofluorescence and electron microscopy
Echocardiogram with strain imaging to evaluate for amyloid cardiomyopathy
Bone marrow biopsy and aspirate to investigate monoclonal gammopathy
Serum albumin and lipid panel to assess degree of nephrotic syndrome
Cardiac MRI if echocardiography is inconclusive for pericardial constriction or infiltrative disease
